page_content:
NUCLEAR ENGINEERING MINOR

Mechanical Engineering (ENME) 2181 Engineering Classroom Building Phone: 301-405-0279 http://enme.umd.edu/undergraduate/degrees/minor-nuclear- engineering/

Contact Dr. Patrick McCluskey (mcclupa@umd.edu) for further information.

The minor in Nuclear Engineering provides the engineering student with the understanding of nuclear engineering and its application to many different ﬁelds, such as power generation, reactor operation, and industrial uses. Students in the minor will learn the fundamentals of nuclear reactor engineering, radiation interactions and measurement, power plant design concepts, and reactor safety. The minor is open to any student in the Clark School of Engineering.

REQUIREMENTS To successfully complete the minor in nuclear engineering, a student must complete a total of 15 credits (5 3-credit courses). All courses must be completed with a grade of "C-" or higher. A maximum of two of the required ﬁve courses can be used to satisfy requirements of the student's major (with approval of the major department).
